outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Channel names, actor claims utilities, and the broadcast

engineering_coverage.applicable: Single PR; but larger migration surface increases conflict risk

stage_payload.decision_summary: Reception and the Prime messaging app maintain parallel definitions of three coupling points that have no compile-time enforcement: Prime channel names, HMAC actor-claims utilities, and the whole-hostel broadcast channel ID. The decision is which extraction strategy gives the best compile-time safety with the lowest restructuring cost, given that both apps already depend on `@acme/lib`.
goals: Compile-time enforcement for the three highest-risk cross-app coupling points
non_goals: Extracting Prime's internal message-type graph (`MessageKind`, `MessageAudience`, etc.)
Unifying the full inbox API models (reception's intentional type widening stays)
Changing any API shape or wire format

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Divergence currently fails silently at runtime
Minimal disruption to both apps' internal type graphs
Round-trip test for actor claims is currently absent
Actor claims HMAC is load-bearing; regression = broken inbox
Must be revertable in one PR

options_considered.Description: Move all Prime response types to `@acme/lib/prime`: channels, actor claims, `PrimeReviewThreadSummary`, `PrimeReviewThreadDetail`, `PrimeReviewCampaignDetail`, broadcast ID
options_considered.Upside: All shared language in one place; maximum compile-time coverage
options_considered.Downside: Requires importing prime-internal message types (`MessageKind`, `MessageAudience`, `MessageAttachment`, `MessageLink`, `MessageCard`) into `@acme/lib`; large surface increase
options_considered.Key risks: Complex deps in shared package; reception's intentional type widening would need to be abandoned or replicated via mapped types
options_considered.Viable?: No — complexity cost exceeds safety gain

options_considered.Description: Keep all parallel definitions; add TypeScript `satisfies` assertions in a cross-app test file
options_considered.Upside: Zero refactoring risk; zero migration surface
options_considered.Downside: Does not give compile-time enforcement for actor claims (runtime HMAC breakage stays silent at compile time); structural tests only catch type drift, not algorithm drift
options_considered.Key risks: Algorithm divergence in actor claims would still compile
options_considered.Viable?: No — insufficient for the highest-risk coupling point (actor claims)

options_considered.Description: Extract to `@acme/lib/prime`: channel const, actor claims sign+verify, broadcast ID constant. Add `satisfies` tests for complex types that stay in-app
options_considered.Upside: Compile-time guarantee for the three highest-risk points; follows existing `@acme/lib/hospitality` pattern; minimal restructuring
options_considered.Downside: Complex types (`PrimeReviewThreadSummary`, etc.) still duplicated (mitigated by structural tests)
options_considered.Key risks: Actor claims migration must preserve byte-identical serialization; one wrong change breaks all mutating Prime calls

rejected_approaches: **Option A (full extraction)** — extracting `PrimeReviewCampaignDetail` and detail types
**Option B (structural tests only)** — structural tests do not catch algorithm divergence in
